title: Scholarium vs Academia.edu Auth and Publication Benchmark
objective: Compare Scholarium publication auth and migration architecture against Academia.edu
primary_subject: Scholarium auth and Academia.edu gap analysis
task_intent: Assess current Scholarium auth maturity, Google/OpenAI entry parity, Codex/Antigravity WebAuth architecture, and Academia.edu feature gaps
product_hypothesis: Scholarium can differentiate with proof-first publication, transparent ranking, provider-gated project starters, and Education-suite handoffs
repo_summary: OpenAI ChatGPT identity uses platform headers
Google OAuth uses PKCE/state encrypted cookies
Provider sessions do not auto-merge emails
Integrations are pending-consent records, not full provider OAuth handoffs
Project starter is provider-gated and provenance-first
research_questions: What auth and publishing capabilities are already present?
What is weaker in Google/Antigravity than OpenAI/Codex?
What does Academia.edu provide that Scholarium lacks?
Which differentiators should be architected next?
source_index: Academia.edu official support and product pages
